subject: 🏃 Sunday — Cycling 60 min or Hike 90 min
html:
Today: Cross-training — cycling 60 min easy OR a 90-min hike. Dealer's choice. Both keep aerobic fitness ticking without loading the knee. If you ride, keep it conversational — HR under 135. If you hike, Rancho San Antonio is a great option with some real elevation to make it count. Either way, don't forget your daily mobility flush (~12 min): T-spine extensions (PT), standing lunges with hip IR (PT), world's greatest stretch, 90/90, couch stretch, hip CARs, cat-cow.

Focus cue: During the mobility flush, spend extra time on 90/90 hip stretches — really lean into the internal rotation side. That bilateral IR limitation is the #1 thing we're rebuilding, and the daily reps are what move the needle.

Context: This wraps Week 1 of 4 in Chassis Build — 3 weeks to your PT follow-up on May 15, 91 days to race day. Your knee was Green across every session this week: Hip & Core A Monday, Upper Push Tuesday, Hip & Core B Thursday, and Friday's 3.57-mile run. That's the cleanest week you've had since this plan started. Bulgarian split squat at 15 lb/hand cleared Good with no knee complaint — real signal the chassis work is already landing. Tomorrow kicks off Week 2: Hip & Core A, with a bump in isometric hold times (+5 sec). Enjoy today.
